scenario: Keystone Bank processes 16000 transactions per day in Camunda 8.8. The service task call-payment-gateway may throw the BPMN error code GATEWAY_DOWN. When that error fires, the current activity must end and the token must flow through the boundary error handler branch without continuing past the original task.
question: How is the boundary error event configured to interrupt the attached service task?

options.text: Use an interrupting boundary error event with the matching error code; the attached activity is terminated and the boundary path is taken
Use a non-interrupting boundary error event with the matching error code; the attached activity continues in parallel with the handler branch
Use an interrupting boundary timer event with the same error code; timer events can match named BPMN errors when configured properly
Use an interrupting boundary message event whose name equals GATEWAY_DOWN; the engine resolves error codes by message name on activities

a.text: Correct. Boundary error events with matching error codes are interrupting by definition; the attached activity is cancelled and execution continues on the boundary path.

b.text: Incorrect. Boundary error events do not support a non-interrupting variant in Camunda; error catch is always interrupting.

c.text: Incorrect. Timer events match elapsed time, not BPMN error codes; they cannot intercept a thrown error from a service task.

d.text: Incorrect. Message events match message names through correlation; error codes are matched by error event definitions, not by message names.

explanation: Boundary error events in Camunda are interrupting and match the thrown error code; the attached activity is cancelled and execution continues on the boundary path.
